original_hypothesis.title: mint() and burn() Bypass Pause and Blacklist Checks Allowing Supply Manipulation During Emergency
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: mint() and burn() only check _operating flag but NOT paused() state or blacklist status. When the contract is paused for an emergency, minters can still create and destroy tokens. Additionally, tokens can be minted to blacklisted/sanctioned addresses, violating compliance controls.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: For a regulated EUR stablecoin, the ability to mint to sanctioned addresses or manipulate supply during an emergency pause represents a critical compliance gap. The burn() NatSpec explicitly claims 'Validates that sender is not blacklisted' but no such check exists.

proof_of_signal.pattern: mint() checks hasRole + _operating + allowance but NOT paused() or isBlacklisted(to); burn() checks hasRole + _operating but NOT paused() or isBlacklisted(_msgSender())

preconditions: Minter or MASTER_MINTER role is compromised or colluding
Contract is paused and/or target address is blacklisted

initial_code_evidence.vulnerable_pattern: No call to adminSanity, no paused() check, no isBlacklisted() check in either function
initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: Supply manipulation continues during emergency pause; blacklisted addresses can receive minted tokens
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: Missing: whenNotPaused modifier and blacklist validation on to/sender

analysis_questions: Can a minter mint while contract is paused and then transfer once unpaused?
Does minting to a blacklisted address create compliance liability?
Is the safety switch (_operating=false) sufficient protection?
validation_requirements: Test: pause contract, call mint - verify it succeeds
Test: blacklist address X, call mint(X, amount) - verify it succeeds
Test: burn from blacklisted minter - verify it succeeds

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: Admin pauses the contract during an emergency. A compromised minter calls mint(attacker, 1000000) which succeeds because mint doesn't check paused(). When contract is unpaused, attacker has inflated tokens ready to sell.

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Unauthorized token minting during emergency; compliance violation by minting to sanctioned addresses
potential_impact_if_confirmed.affected_users: All token holders affected by supply inflation; regulatory exposure for protocol
potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: Admin can forceTransfer back or burn excess tokens if minter cooperates

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: No test for mint/burn during paused state or to blacklisted addresses

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
